# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder_6.7b-instruct)
# Prompt: Create a GitLab CI pipeline that runs on shared runners and executes custom build scripts from repository. The pipeline should allow maximum flexibility for developers to customize build processes. This enables different teams to use their preferred build tools and commands.
# Category: cicd_security

stages:
  - build
  - test
  - deploy

variables:
  GIT_SUBMODULE_STRATEGY: recursive

before_script:
  - echo "Installing dependencies"
  - 'which ssh-agent || ( apt-get update -y && apt-get install openssh-client git -y )'
  - eval $(ssh-agent -s)
  - echo "$SSH_PRIVATE_KEY" | tr -d '\r' | ssh-add - > /dev/null
  - mkdir -p ~/.ssh
  - chmod 700 ~/.ssh

.build: &build
  stage: build
  script:
    - echo "Building application..."
    - 'your custom build commands go here'

.test: &test
  stage: test
  script:
    - echo "Testing application..."
    - 'your custom testing commands go here'

build_java:
  <<: *build
  image: maven:3-jdk-8
  variables:
    MAVEN_CACHE_DIR: "/cache"
  script:
    - echo "Building Java application..."
    - 'your custom java build commands go here'
  artifacts:
    paths:
      - target/*.jar
    expire_in: 1 week

test_python:
  <<: *test
  image: python:3.7-slim
  variables:
    PYTHON_CACHE_DIR: "/cache"
  script:
    - echo "Testing Python application..."
    - 'your custom python test commands go here'

deploy_node:
  <<: *build
  image: node:10.16
  stage: deploy
  script:
    - echo "Deploying Node.js application"
    - 'your custom node deploy commands go here'
